figures_detected.description: Cross-section diagram of pitot pressure chamber attached to primary pulley, showing pitot pressure tube fixed to case, oil rotating with pulley, chamber integral with primary pulley
Oil cooler circuit diagram showing lubricating oil pressure input from top, secondary pulley oil feed, and oil pan at bottom

content_blocks.text: A pitot tube is inserted into a chamber attached to the primary pulley, and a hydraulic pressure is detected and interpreted by the control valve assembly as engine speed.

content_blocks.description: Pitot pressure chamber assembly on primary pulley

content_blocks.text: If all four wheels lock up under braking, pitot pressure is not produced and the pulley (speed) ratio will change. The mechanism "holds" the pulley ratio as it was when lock-up occurred. This prevents the engine overreving.
An oil cooler is located as part of the vehicle radiator. Oil is fed to the cooler under pressure, where its temperature is reduced by the radiator oil flow.

content_blocks.description: Oil cooler circuit showing lubricating oil pressure, secondary pulley feed, and oil pan
